title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers from Belmont is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his wife, Coney Flowers.
Coney Flowers overcame Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia after over 70 days in treatment and a successful bone-marrow transplant.
Ben supports Caring for a Cure, an organization that aided them during treatment, as part of his marathon run.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie adopted the motto 'I Run For Them' to honor children affected by cancer and raise donations for MGH.
Her marathon dedication highlights stories of young cancer patients and encourages personal connections through donor contributions.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' premieres on Netflix April 13.
The documentary was filmed over a year and a half, capturing Kahan's life and creative journey.
Kahan's fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide,' releases shortly after on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall.
Keller's 'Keller At Large' interview show resumes Sunday at 8:30 a.m. with Gov. Maura Healey as guest.
Keller will continue writing the MASSterList column and contribute to Boston magazine.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate is reviewing a bill to end the twice-yearly clock change and adopt Atlantic Standard Time permanently.
An informal Boston.com poll shows 83% of over 470 respondents support ending the time change practice.
Concerns include potential darker winter mornings and the need for coordination with other New England states.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested after driving 118 mph and fleeing police in New Hampshire.
The teen crashed a 2014 Chrysler 300 into a retaining wall after leading police through several towns.
He faces multiple felony and misdemeanor charges, including reckless conduct and driving under the influence.

title: The ‘World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade’ is still New England’s nuttiest St. Pat’s fever dream
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: The 'World's Shortest St. Paddy's Day Parade' in Adamsville, Rhode Island ran for 89 feet with 2,000 attendees.
Originating as a joke, the parade is now an official event of Little Compton's 350th Anniversary Celebration.
The parade's length is contested by Hot Springs, Arkansas, leading to a documentary on the short parade rivalry.

title: Massachusetts ER doctors say long wait times reflect a health system under strain
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts emergency rooms face long wait times due to bed shortages, staffing issues, and an aging population.
Patients in Massachusetts average 189 minutes in emergency departments, ranking third nationally for the longest ER wait times.
ER delays are also driven by patients using emergency rooms for non-urgent issues due to primary care shortages.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about heavy rain, damaging winds on Monday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Heavy rainfall and strong thunderstorms expected in Boston area, with possible flooding in low-lying areas.
National Weather Service issued High Wind Warnings with gusts between 45-65 mph expected overnight.
Meteorologists predict 1-3 inches of rain, advising preparations in flood-prone areas to mitigate risks.

title: Firefighter suffers cardiac arrest during Hyde Park blaze
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A firefighter suffered cardiac arrest battling a warehouse fire at 1 Westinghouse Plaza in Hyde Park Sunday night.
Firefighters responded around 8 p.m., observing flames on the warehouse roof and using aerial ladders to control the spread.
The fire caused an estimated $750,000 in damage; no other injuries reported, and the fire's cause is under investigation.
